speaker: DUNCAN
text: What bloody man is that? He can report,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: As seemeth by his plight, of the revolt

speaker: DUNCAN
text: The newest state.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: This is the sergeant

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Who, like a good and hardy soldier, fought

speaker: MALCOLM
text: ’Gainst my captivity.—Hail, brave friend!

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Say to the King the knowledge of the broil

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As thou didst leave it.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Doubtful it stood,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: As two spent swimmers that do cling together

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And choke their art. The merciless Macdonwald

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: (Worthy to be a rebel, for to that

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: The multiplying villainies of nature

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Do swarm upon him) from the Western Isles

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Of kerns and gallowglasses is supplied;

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And Fortune, on his damnèd quarrel smiling,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Showed like a rebel’s whore. But all’s too weak;

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: For brave Macbeth (well he deserves that name),

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Disdaining Fortune, with his brandished steel,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Which smoked with bloody execution,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Like Valor’s minion, carved out his passage

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Till he faced the slave;

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Which ne’er shook hands, nor bade farewell to him,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Till he unseamed him from the nave to th’ chops,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And fixed his head upon our battlements.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: O valiant cousin, worthy gentleman!

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: As whence the sun ’gins his reflection

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Shipwracking storms and direful thunders break,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: So from that spring whence comfort seemed to come

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Discomfort swells. Mark, King of Scotland, mark:

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: No sooner justice had, with valor armed,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Compelled these skipping kerns to trust their heels,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: But the Norweyan lord, surveying vantage,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: With furbished arms and new supplies of men,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Began a fresh assault.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Dismayed not this our captains, Macbeth and Banquo?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Yes, as sparrows eagles, or the hare the lion.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: If I say sooth, I must report they were

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: As cannons overcharged with double cracks,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: So they doubly redoubled strokes upon the foe.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Except they meant to bathe in reeking wounds

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Or memorize another Golgotha,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I cannot tell—

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: But I am faint. My gashes cry for help.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: So well thy words become thee as thy wounds:

speaker: DUNCAN
text: They smack of honor both.—Go, get him surgeons.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Who comes here?

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The worthy Thane of Ross.

speaker: LENNOX
text: What a haste looks through his eyes!

speaker: LENNOX
text: So should he look that seems to speak things strange.

speaker: ROSS
text: God save the King.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Whence cam’st thou, worthy thane?

speaker: ROSS
text: From Fife, great king,

speaker: ROSS
text: Where the Norweyan banners flout the sky

speaker: ROSS
text: And fan our people cold.

speaker: ROSS
text: Norway himself, with terrible numbers,

speaker: ROSS
text: Assisted by that most disloyal traitor,

speaker: ROSS
text: The Thane of Cawdor, began a dismal conflict,

speaker: ROSS
text: Till that Bellona’s bridegroom, lapped in proof,

speaker: ROSS
text: Confronted him with self-comparisons,

speaker: ROSS
text: Point against point, rebellious arm ’gainst arm,

speaker: ROSS
text: Curbing his lavish spirit. And to conclude,

speaker: ROSS
text: The victory fell on us.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Great happiness!

speaker: ROSS
text: That now Sweno,

speaker: ROSS
text: The Norways’ king, craves composition.

speaker: ROSS
text: Nor would we deign him burial of his men

speaker: ROSS
text: Till he disbursèd at Saint Colme’s Inch

speaker: ROSS
text: Ten thousand dollars to our general use.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: No more that Thane of Cawdor shall deceive

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Our bosom interest. Go, pronounce his present death,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And with his former title greet Macbeth.

speaker: ROSS
text: I’ll see it done.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: What he hath lost, noble Macbeth hath won.
